enforcement_stack.purpose: Declares legal edges between apps, services, packages, and tools.
Blocks deep imports into sibling internals and private subpaths.
Fails route, shell, gateway, and owner drift before scaffold prompts run.
Makes ownership changes reviewable instead of tacit.
Keeps HTML atlas, parity tables, and selection behavior deterministic.

rules.allowed_access: Every repo path must resolve to exactly one owner context or one shared-kernel class.
rules.description: Closes the starter-shape ambiguity by making path ownership machine-checkable before scaffold work begins.

rules.allowed_access: Every route family must resolve to one topology owner, even when the route label is still derived.
rules.description: Closes route-family ownership drift before apps are scaffolded.

rules.allowed_access: Every shell family must resolve to exactly one baseline app or one explicitly conditional reserved workspace.
rules.description: Closes the omission of support, pharmacy, governance, and assistive shell representation in the starter shape.

rules.allowed_access: Shell apps may only import published shared packages and contract bundles.
rules.description: No app may import domain package internals, private services, or raw representation mappers.

rules.allowed_access: Shell code may render projections, issue commands through contracts, and preserve continuity only.
rules.description: Closes the app-owns-truth gap by explicitly reserving write-model ownership to packages and services.

rules.allowed_access: Shared code is legal only in explicit versioned packages, never in a generic utils folder.
rules.description: Closes the shared-util loophole by freezing the only legal shared namespaces.

rules.allowed_access: Gateway code may publish route-scoped compute boundaries only from contracts and policy packages.
rules.description: Prevents the gateway from reaching into domain internals or becoming a hidden truth owner.

rules.allowed_access: Command API is the only runtime service that may compose multiple domain packages, and only through public entrypoints.
rules.description: Freezes the authoritative mutation seam before feature logic lands.

rules.allowed_access: Projection worker may rebuild derived reads and representations only.
rules.description: Prevents projection freshness, FHIR mapping, or stale-read posture from becoming hidden write authority.

rules.allowed_access: Notification worker may orchestrate effects, receipts, and resend seams only.
rules.description: Prevents the worker from becoming a convenience vendor wrapper that silently settles business truth.

rules.allowed_access: Simulators may model provider seams and proof envelopes only.
rules.description: Ensures local labs cannot replace live-provider proof or mutate domain truth directly.

rules.allowed_access: Domain packages may use the kernel, published event contracts, policy, and telemetry contracts only.
rules.description: Sibling domain internals remain forbidden; cross-context seams publish through contracts instead.

rules.allowed_access: Published route, event, and API truth comes only from explicit contract packages.
rules.description: No ad hoc DTO folder or route-local type may supersede published contracts.

rules.allowed_access: Publication, approval-freeze, and watch tuples bind release truth across shells and services.
rules.description: No consumer may restitch route, event, design, and release truth independently.

rules.allowed_access: FHIR mapping may consume public domain representation entrypoints only.
rules.description: Derived mapping may never write or own canonical lifecycle truth.

rules.allowed_access: Every shell must inherit the shared design system and automation marker graph from day one.
rules.description: Closes the placeholder-shell drift where shells look identical or ship without stable markers.

rules.allowed_access: Tooling may read and validate manifests, docs, and contracts only.
rules.description: Scripts, browser tests, and architecture docs may not become hidden runtime truth owners.

rules.allowed_access: Browser verification is mandatory for topology surfaces and later shell scaffolds.
rules.description: Freezes Playwright as a baseline deliverable instead of a late smoke-test afterthought.

rules.allowed_access: Standalone assistive work is reserved for evaluation, replay, monitoring, or release control only.
rules.description: Prevents a future assistive shell from silently widening into live-care ownership before later prompts publish concrete route contracts.

contracts.title: Shell apps consume published browser/runtime contracts only
contracts.write_model_rule: No shell may reach through API contracts into domain or service private internals.
contracts.notes: This seam carries typed browser/runtime contracts, never canonical domain objects.

contracts.title: Shell apps inherit one shared design and marker bundle
contracts.write_model_rule: Design inheritance may change posture and automation markers but never mint business truth.
contracts.notes: Keeps placeholder shells visually distinct while preserving one product family and one marker graph.

contracts.title: Shell apps consume runtime publication and release posture through one package family
contracts.write_model_rule: Shells may render read-only, recovery-only, or writable posture only when release controls say so.
contracts.notes: Binds route, release, parity, and recovery truth into one publication seam.

contracts.title: API Gateway publishes route-scoped compute from shared contracts
contracts.write_model_rule: Gateway never owns or mutates domain truth; it enforces policy and publication only.
contracts.notes: The gateway stays contract-first and release-aware instead of calling domain internals directly.

contracts.title: API Gateway enforces acting-scope and authz policy through the published policy package
contracts.write_model_rule: Gateway evaluates policy; it never writes access grants, claims, or tenant truth.
contracts.notes: This keeps the browser edge fenced by declared policy tuples instead of route-local shortcuts.

contracts.title: Command API is the only cross-context mutation coordinator
contracts.write_model_rule: Command API may coordinate mutations, but only through public package entrypoints and settlement law.
contracts.notes: Cross-context writes remain explicit and reviewable instead of spreading across apps or sibling services.

contracts.title: Command API publishes authoritative downstream events through one event contract package
contracts.write_model_rule: The command path may emit only versioned canonical events, never ad hoc local DTOs.
contracts.notes: Keeps mutation, settlement, and projection replay aligned to one published event catalogue.

contracts.title: Projection Worker consumes published domain events and public projection seams only
contracts.write_model_rule: Projection worker may derive read truth but never settle canonical state.
contracts.notes: All read models stay downstream of immutable events and published contracts.

contracts.title: Projection Worker reaches FHIR mapping only through the derived representation boundary
contracts.write_model_rule: FHIR representations remain derived and one-way from domain settlements.
contracts.notes: This seam is explicit so partner exchange scaffolds cannot bypass the canonical event spine later.

contracts.title: Notification Worker consumes communications, support, and identity seams as bounded effect contracts
contracts.write_model_rule: Delivery evidence, callback, and resend logic stay explicit; no worker may claim business finality from a partial provider receipt.
contracts.notes: Ties notification scaffolding back to the manual checkpoint and retry law from seq_039 and seq_040.

contracts.title: Adapter simulators consume published contracts and fixture builders only
contracts.write_model_rule: Simulator responses are bounded evidence and parity aids, never live-provider truth.
contracts.notes: Closes the mock-now vs live-later drift by giving simulators an explicit contract-only seam.

contracts.title: Every domain package consumes one shared kernel
contracts.write_model_rule: Cross-context primitives may live only in the kernel, not in sibling domain internals.
contracts.notes: This seam is the only legal shared primitive path across domain packages.

contracts.title: Every domain package publishes through one event contract family
contracts.write_model_rule: Domains publish and consume versioned events; they do not deep-import sibling models.
contracts.notes: Creates one machine-readable seam for every cross-context event handoff.

contracts.title: Domain packages consume policy and telemetry through published packages only
contracts.write_model_rule: Policy and telemetry can constrain or observe domain behavior, but never replace domain-owned aggregates.
contracts.notes: Explicitly blocks the informal shared-utils path for policy helpers or telemetry wrappers.

contracts.title: Release control consumes trust and readiness through observability contracts
contracts.write_model_rule: Release controls may freeze posture based on watch and readiness proof, not on local optimism.
contracts.notes: Binds publication parity to watch tuples and assurance evidence before later release scaffolding lands.

contracts.title: Docs, analysis, architecture rules, and Playwright consume manifests rather than runtime internals
contracts.write_model_rule: Tooling reads manifests and docs only; it cannot become a backdoor runtime owner.
contracts.notes: Keeps validation and browser coverage first-class without widening tooling into business logic.

contracts.title: Standalone assistive work is reserved behind contract and release-control fences
contracts.write_model_rule: Conditional assistive work may evaluate, replay, or observe only; live care assist must remain inside the owning shell.
contracts.notes: Makes the assistive shell decision explicit without prematurely scaffolding a baseline app.
